title: Python Script Starter Template
when_to_use: When starting any Python automation script for offensive work

steps.title: Shebang
steps.tip: env python3 is more portable than /usr/bin/python3 as Python location varies

steps.title: Import Essentials
steps.instruction: Import essentials: import sys, os, socket, datetime
steps.tip: Only import what you need. sys for args, os for files, socket for network, datetime for timestamps

steps.title: Argument Handling
steps.instruction: Add argument handling: if len(sys.argv) < 2: print('Usage: script.py <target>'); sys.exit(1)
steps.tip: For complex arguments, use argparse instead. For simple scripts, sys.argv is sufficient

steps.title: Main Function
steps.tip: Keep main() clean. Call other functions from main(). This keeps code organised and testable

steps.title: Error Handling
steps.instruction: Add try/except for error handling around all network calls
steps.tip: Catch specific exceptions: socket.gaierror for DNS, socket.timeout for timeouts, ConnectionRefusedError for refused

steps.title: Output Function
steps.instruction: Add output function: write results to timestamped file
steps.tip: Use f-strings for clean formatting. Include run metadata: target, timestamp, script version

steps.title: Entry Point
steps.tip: This pattern allows importing functions from other scripts without executing main()
